steps.title: Identify the growth pattern
steps.content: Each month the enrollment is $4\%$ greater than the previous month, so the new total is $1.04$ times the old total. This is exponential growth with base $1.04$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: Starting with $200$ customers in January 2018 (when $m = 0$), after $m$ months the total is $$c = 200(1.04)^{m}.$$

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content:
• A) $c = 200(0.04)^{m}$: base $0.04$ would shrink the total each month. Eliminated.
  • B) $c = 200(1.04)^{m}$: correctly applies a $4\%$ monthly increase.
  • C) $c = 200(1.4)^{m}$: represents a $40\%$ monthly increase, not $4\%$. Eliminated.
  • D) $c = 200(4)^{m}$: would quadruple the total each month. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
